schema.name: TZPID source truth promoted from reviewed L2 recovery candidate
schema.basis: reviewed D-drive source evidence; local candidate preview code bleed stripped

dictionary_sections.definition: The Bayesian Evidence Accumulation Framework is the registry rule for comparing candidate explanations by posterior odds, using likelihood evidence and prior model weights.
dictionary_sections.contextual_meaning: Within the TZPID corpus, this entry gives the review lane a disciplined way to compare competing hypotheses: evidence raises or lowers relative support, while priors keep the framework from rewarding complexity without sufficient likelihood gain.
dictionary_sections.derivable_consequences: The relation supports model comparison, parameter-estimation discipline, falsifiability review, and experimental prioritization.
dictionary_sections.lemma_support: Posterior odds identity; likelihood ratio; prior odds; Bayesian model comparison.
dictionary_sections.technical_interpretation: The entry formalizes Bayesian model comparison for the registry. Evidence updates the relative credibility of hypotheses while priors can penalize unnecessary model complexity.
dictionary_sections.equation_grounding: Grounded in the source ID047 Bayesian Evidence Accumulation Framework packet.
dictionary_sections.interpretive_role: This entry replaces an artifact-title recovery packet with the clean source equation already present in the early TZP canon.
dictionary_sections.tzp_thesaurus: Bayesian evidence; posterior odds; likelihood ratio; prior odds; model comparison; falsifiability.
dictionary_sections.encyclopedia_mathematica: Mathematically, ID2972 is the posterior-odds form of Bayes' theorem. Its support layer belongs to probability, statistical inference, model selection, and evidence accumulation.

publication_overrides.equation_support_summary: Artifact title removed; Bayesian posterior-odds equation normalized from source canon.

wolfram.note: Carrier form preserves source LaTeX as Wolfram-readable data; semantic proof remains domain-specific.

theory.canonical_statement: Competing Trawinistic hypotheses are compared by posterior odds: the posterior ratio equals the likelihood ratio multiplied by the prior odds.
theory.technical_interpretation: The entry formalizes Bayesian model comparison for the registry. Evidence updates the relative credibility of hypotheses while priors can penalize unnecessary model complexity.

reviewed_source_symbols.meaning: observed data or evidence used in the update
posterior probability of hypothesis H_i after observing D
likelihood of the data under hypothesis H_i
prior probability assigned to hypothesis H_i

source_truth_additions.note: Additive promotion packet retained without removing existing source-truth content.

proof_pipeline.status: source-truth provenance linked to generated proof-obligation and certificate artifacts
proof_pipeline.scope_note: This metadata records authorship, lineage, and proof-pipeline artifact links. It does not by itself assert that the entry has a completed native analytic proof.

verification_summary.wolfram_spine_checks: passed for generated curated/Zenodo spine checks; energy-matter naive integral divergence warning is expected

normalization_notes: Carrier form preserves exact LaTeX as Wolfram string data
Semantic/numeric interpretation remains a later domain-specific upgrade

carrier_semantics.claim: The block is a valid Wolfram carrier for the source equation text; it is not yet a domain-specific computational proof.

wolfram_representation.scope: per-ID numeric atom and symbolic token representation with best-effort Wolfram candidates

blocks.wolfram_input: HoldForm[TZPIDWolframRepresentation["ID2972", 1, <|"kind" -> "symbolic_token_index", "title" -> "Bayesian Evidence Accumulation Framework", "latex" -> "\\frac{P(H_i\\mid D)}{P(H_j\\mid D)}=\\frac{P(D\\mid H_i)}{P(D\\mid H_j)}\\frac{P(H_i)}{P(H_j)}", "numericAtoms" -> {}, "symbolicTokens" -> {"mid", "P", "H", "j", "D"}, "candidateWolframInput" -> "", "candidateMethod" -> "untranslated_latex_command"|>]]

summary.candidate_policy: best_effort_candidates_parse_checked; unparseable candidates downgraded to symbolic token representation
